description: Canonical eval cases for the Logistics Agent. Pins mode-selection discipline + bonded-warehouse explanation + customs-clearance grounding.

cases.name: Mode selection · urgent + light
cases.input: Need 200 kg of consumer electronics from Shenzhen to Frankfurt in 7 days. Sea or air?
cases.description: Urgent + light + high-value → air freight is the obvious answer. Agent must surface a concrete recommendation with cost, not hedge.

cases.name: Bonded warehouse · re-export use case
cases.input: We import samples that we sometimes re-export back to Asia for QC iteration. Should we be using a bonded warehouse?
cases.description: Re-export samples are the canonical bonded-warehouse use case — duty + VAT are never paid. Agent must make this point clearly.

cases.name: Routing · sea freight cost to two EU ports
cases.input: I'm shipping a 20ft container of furniture from Ningbo. Is it cheaper to bring it into Rotterdam or Gdańsk for onward delivery to Poland?
cases.description: A routing comparison must be grounded in the freight calculator — concrete € figures per lane, not a generic 'it depends'. Tests that the agent uses the routing tool rather than hand-waving.

cases.name: LCL vs FCL · consolidation break-even
cases.input: We ship about 12 cubic metres of goods from Shenzhen every month. Should we keep using LCL (less than container load) or move to a full 20ft container?
cases.description: At ~12cbm/month the LCL-vs-FCL break-even is live (a 20ft holds ~28-33cbm). The agent must reason on the per-cbm economics with figures, not give a generic answer.

cases.name: Bonded warehouse · seasonal SKU cash-flow trade-off
cases.input: We import Christmas decorations in August but only start selling them in November. A 40ft container, €60,000 cargo value. Bonded storage vs. clearing on arrival?
cases.description: Three-month seasonal hold + €60k value → bonded warehousing's duty/VAT deferral usually wins on working capital terms. The agent must show the cash-flow math + bonded storage cost + decision.

cases.name: Insurance · ICC clauses + war/strikes endorsement
cases.input: We're shipping €180k of electronics ex-Vietnam. Cargo insurance broker quoted us 0.18% premium under ICC(A). Is that enough coverage for a tech consignment crossing pirate-risk waters?
cases.description: ICC(A) is broadest standard cover; war/strikes is a separate endorsement. The €180k cargo value must appear (mustContainNumbers — apex P1.6); the agent must explain what's covered + flag the war/strikes gap, not just assert coverage.

cases.name: Lead time · buffer-stock math for 6-week ocean transit
cases.input: Sea freight from Shenzhen to Rotterdam is currently running 38-45 days. We sell 800 units/week. How much buffer stock should I hold to avoid stockouts during a transit slip?
cases.description: Buffer-stock math: weekly demand × max-vs-min lead-time variance. The agent must compute the buffer figure, not give a generic 'hold more stock' answer.
